sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.64 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: Is it true that, in a group of the form$G=AB$, every subgroup$N$of$A B$which is subnormal both in$A$and in$B$is subnormal in$G$? The answer is affirmative in the case of finite groups (H.\,Wielandt). { Ya.\,P.\,Sysak} No, not always (C.\,Casolo, U.\,Dardano, J. Group Theory, { 7} (2004), 507--520).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
